all_connects, and update_time fields.

Fix WSL2 session path mismatch for --continue and session history

Electron runs as a Windows process but Claude Code runs inside WSL2,
causing session lookups to use wrong home dir and path encoding.
Added helpers to resolve WSL home via UNC path and encode CWD as
WSL-style path. Fixes --continue not finding sessions and potentially
session history showing incomplete results.

commit.message:
Bootstrap eval: B=30, 5reg+5cls, crash-safe output with metadata

- Change defaults: n_bootstrap=50→30, n_reg=10→5, n_cls=10→5
- Add --n-reg / --n-cls CLI arguments for flexible configuration
- Add metadata sidecar JSON (bootstrap_meta_*.json) alongside each
  final CSV, recording all params, methods, timing, hostname
- Never overwrite: existing results with different params are archived
  to {output_dir}/_prev/ with timestamped directories
- Checkpoint stores config (B, n_reg, n_cls, seed): on resume, stale
  checkpoints from a different config are detected and archived
- Orphaned partial CSVs (from power-loss before first checkpoint) are
  detected and archived to prevent header/column mismatch corruption
- finalize_output validates rows: truncated rows from mid-append power
  loss are discarded (empty boot_id/method check)
- Cleanup of intermediate files happens ONLY after both final CSV and
  metadata sidecar are safely on disk — no gap for power-loss to leave
  an unrecoverable state
- Remove SVR/SVC from multi_model_evaluator (unused, slow)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use SHCreateDefaultContextMenu with explicit registry keys to load all extension handlers

Replace IShellFolder::GetUIObjectOf with SHCreateDefaultContextMenu —
the same function Explorer uses internally. The key difference is that
DEFCONTEXTMENU.aKeys lets us pass the registry keys (HKCR\*, HKCR\<ext>,
HKCR\<ProgID>, HKCR\AllFilesystemObjects) that the shell uses to enumerate
dynamic context menu handlers. Without these, only static verbs from the
IShellFolder itself appear (e.g. "Run as administrator" from HKCR\exefile\
shell\runas), while COM-based extension handlers like 7-Zip and Notepad++
(registered under HKCR\*\shellex\ContextMenuHandlers) are skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix WQP cron: expand lookback to 12 months and add missing characteristics

State agencies upload to WQP with 6-12 month lag — the 6-month window
only captured 19 records for MD. With 12 months: 7,323 records.

Added characteristics that state agencies actually use:
- Total Nitrogen mixed forms (NH3/NH4/NO2/NO3), Nitrate + Nitrite
- Total Phosphorus mixed forms, Orthophosphate
- Enterococcus (5,112 MD records alone), Fecal Coliform
- Conductivity, TSS, Chlorophyll a

commit.message:
fix: pass hwndHost to GetUIObjectOf, add SetForegroundWindow + OleInitialize

Three fixes:

1. Create helper window BEFORE GetUIObjectOf and pass it as hwndOwner.
   Some shell extensions (7-Zip etc.) inspect hwndOwner during context
   menu creation and silently skip their items when it is null or from
   a foreign process.

2. Call SetForegroundWindow(hwndHost) before TrackPopupMenuEx. Without
   this the menu's internal message loop never receives keyboard events,
   so Escape (and all other keys) are ignored.

3. Call OleInitialize/OleUninitialize on the STA thread. Some shell
   handlers require OLE to be fully initialised (not just CoInitialize)
   before they register their menu items. PostMessage(WM_NULL) after
   TrackPopupMenuEx flushes leftover messages per MSDN guidance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix CORS: switch n8n fetch calls to URLSearchParams to avoid preflight

n8n Cloud returns 500 on OPTIONS preflight requests, blocking all POST
requests with JSON bodies from GitHub Pages. Switching to URLSearchParams
(application/x-www-form-urlencoded) sends a simple request with no preflight,
bypassing the CORS block entirely. Affects updateSaleStatus, saveNotes,
and sendFarewellMessage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 12–18 + Kardashev ML: gap fixes, menu v2, synthetic training

Phase 12–17 (previously uncommitted):
- BC11 pattern analysis hardening (Bonferroni, harmonics, non-grav bounds)
- API layer: real persistence, monitoring, batch detection, orbital history
- Data layer: SBDB nongrav fix, observation arc wiring, Horizons history
- Menu redesign: ANEOSMenuV2 (14 options), ANEOSMenuBase helpers
- σ validation integrity audit; honest docstrings; Wilson CIs

Phase 18 — Menu gap analysis + fixes:
- GAP-1/2: aneos_api.services → aneos_api.database; save_analysis_result dict fix
- GAP-3: _show_db_results uses dict .get() (not getattr); column names updated
- GAP-4: run_200_year_poll passes interactive=False (no EOF crash)
- GAP-5: print() in _detect_batch → show_info/show_error
- GAP-7: evidence_breakdown added to DetectionResult.analysis dict
- GAP-8: _parse_elements_table rewritten with epoch accumulation (2 epochs → 2 rows)

- GAP-10: format_sigma σ<2 → INCONCLUSIVE (removes NOTABLE/ROUTINE)
- M-1/2/3: Options 12–14 added (_start_api_server, _detection_analytics, _show_help)

Kardashev Synthetic ML (new):
- aneos_core/datasets/kardashev_generator.py — 14 scenarios K0.5–K2.0
  (dead_rocket_stage, solar_sail, active_ion_probe, mining_tug,

  pipeline; hardcoded ground-truth set (2020 SO, J002E3, WT1190F, Roadster-type,
  Apophis, Bennu, Eros, Toutatis, Itokawa, 2001 YB5)

  score >0.94 P(artificial); all confirmed naturals score <0.46

commit.message:
Phase 2 fix: merge left+right hands before tokenization

REMI is a single-track format. Previously, split_files_for_training
processed each MIDI track independently, producing separate right-hand
and left-hand chunks. The model fine-tuned on those learned single-hand
patterns and generated single-hand output.

Fix: merge all tracks into one before chunking and seeding.
- Added merge_tracks() utility in setup cell
- All 19 corpus MIDIs now produce two-hand chunks (58 train, 5 valid)
- Lost waltz seed is now 1 merged sequence (1148 tokens, was 2x separate)
- Reduced pitch augmentation from ±12 to ±3/±5 semitones
- Added data/chunked/ and data/merged/ to .gitignore (regenerable)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(figma-console-mastery): v1.3.0 — external library import workflows

Add empirically-verified workflows for importing components from external
libraries via the Plugin API, with distinct paths for Team Libraries
(importComponentByKeyAsync) and UI Kits (Clone Pattern).

Key findings from production testing:
- Team Libraries: full import/set/style API works when library is published + enabled
- UI Kits (M3, iOS, Apple): invisible to both Plugin API and REST API; Clone Pattern only
- figma_search_components searches local file only, not external libraries
- teamLibrary API in Desktop Bridge exposes only 2 methods (variable collections)

commit.message:
fix: run shell context menu on STA thread to load all extension handlers

Shell extension handlers (7-Zip, Notepad++, etc.) are in-process STA
COM servers. When IContextMenu is created from an MTA thread (the .NET
console app default), COM marshals calls across apartments and the
handlers silently refuse to load, producing only the generic built-in
menu. Fix: dispatch ShowCore() onto a dedicated STA thread via
Thread.SetApartmentState(STA) so all registered handlers load and
appear correctly.

commit.message:
ci: install golangci-lint via go install to match project Go version

Pre-built golangci-lint binaries are compiled with go1.24, but
go.mod requires go1.25+. Installing via go install compiles the
linter with the project's own toolchain, eliminating the version
mismatch. Also runs go mod tidy which bumped go directive to 1.25.0
(required by a transitive dependency).
